root_cause.summary: The pytest functional test `tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` fails because GitHub returns repeated 502 Bad Gateway errors when pip tries to download `https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip`. The pip subprocess exits with code 1, the test framework asserts on exit code 0, and `AssertionError: Script returned code: 1` is raised. The test was retried 3 times by pytest-rerunfailures before the final fail; the root cause is transient external infrastructure (GitHub 502s), not a bug in pip or in the truststore code path the test name suggests.

required_signals.value: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))

evidence_spans.reason: FAILURES section header + the failing test parametrization (`test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]`).

The full surface-and-root-cause block: `E AssertionError: Script returned code: 1` (the surface), the locals showing the failing pip command and its args, the AssertionError stack location at `tests/lib/__init__.py:681`, and the captured pip stderr `ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443) ... too many 502 error responses` (the actual root cause).

Final test summary: `FAILED tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]`, the `1 failed, 1080 passed, ... 3 rerun` totals confirming this is the single failure and was retried, the nox session error, and the GHA `##[error]Process completed with exit code 1.` annotation.
